author: mark
text:
bl close: resolve delivered_in via tag-scan when null

Deferred-mode `bl review` lands no local squash, so it never writes
the `delivered_in` hint. By the time `bl close` runs after the forge
merges the PR, the field is still null and every later `bl show` has
to re-scan. Extend close to opportunistically resolve and cache it.

- `delivery::populate_on_close` reuses the existing `resolve` tag-scan
  (the [bl-xxxx] subject tag is ground truth; this is a strict
  generalization of the drifted-SHA fallback, not a new mechanism)
  against the task's effective target_branch. A miss warns and
  proceeds — the tag stays authoritative and the half-push detector
  (scans subjects, not this hint) is untouched.
- `bl close --delivered <sha>` overrides the scan unconditionally for
  the forge rebase-merge case (operator points at one commit).
- A newly resolved hint is committed to the state branch before
  close_and_archive git-rm's the file, so archive recovery's
  pre-deletion blob carries it — mirroring how local-squash mode
  persists it back in `review`. Local-squash close is a no-op here
  (hint already set): byte-identical, behavior unchanged.

Decomposition forced by the 300-line cap (convention: sibling module +
re-export): cmd_claim/resolve_claim_policy lifted to commands/claim.rs;
the Close arg block lifted to cli_sub::CloseArgs (mirrors CreateArgs/
SyncArgs); both re-exported so callers stay byte-stable. Reconciled
with concurrently-landed cmd_update→update.rs + reject_deferred.

Child of bl-00ca (forge-gated delivery). Tests: deferred auto-populate,
scan-miss warn-and-proceed, --delivered override; unit coverage for the
manual/no-op/miss branches. Full gate green, 100% coverage.
